snapshot.title: Department Founded
snapshot.shortDesc: Official establishment with initial enrollment of 240 students across three departments.

snapshot.title: First Graduating Class
snapshot.shortDesc: The Department produced its first set of graduating students in 1965. Commencement ceremony celebrates 52 graduates, establishing institutional traditions.

snapshot.title: First Graduating Class
snapshot.shortDesc: The Department produced its first set of graduating students in 1965. Commencement ceremony celebrates 52 graduates, establishing institutional traditions.

snapshot.title: Graduate Studies Started
snapshot.shortDesc: Collaboration with Mathematics and Engineering departments to create joint research programs. This cross-disciplinary approach fostered innovation at the intersection of theory and practice, producing research that influenced both academic thought and industrial application.

snapshot.title: Undergraduate Curriculum Reviewed
snapshot.shortDesc: Complete redesign of the undergraduate program to emphasize software engineering and practical skills. The new curriculum balanced theoretical foundations with hands-on experience, preparing graduates for both research careers and industry positions.

snapshot.title: Center for Energy Research and Development Founded
snapshot.shortDesc: Opening of the Global Computing Research Center, establishing connections with institutions worldwide. This initiative positioned the department as a leader in international collaboration, hosting visiting scholars and facilitating joint research projects across continents.
